name: Ndonga
notes: Ndonga (Oshindonga), the main Ovambo (Bantu) language of northern Namibia and southern Angola. INPUT CONTRACT: the current standard Namibian orthography (Oshindonga omunyolelo / 'Oshindonga Orthography 3', 2004, implemented by NIED) is assumed; text is expected to be already normalised. KEY FEATURES: (1) DIGRAPHS: <dh> = /ð/, <th> = /θ/, <sh> = /ʃ/, <ng> = /ŋ/ (also read /ŋɡ/ in the prenasalised cluster), <ny> = /ɲ/, <mb> = /mb/, <nd> = /nd/, <ndj> = /ndʒ/, <ts> = /ts/. (2) SINGLE LETTERS with non-obvious values: <c> = /ts/, <g> = /ɣ/ (voiced velar fricative, not a stop), <j> = /dʒ/, <q> = /kw/, <x> = /x/, <y> = /j/. (3) VOWEL LENGTH is written by doubling: <aa ee ii oo uu>; the language has a plain five-vowel system /a e i o u/. (4) The full phonetic inventory also contains voiceless nasals and aspirated/ejective prenasalised compounds ([m̥p], [n̥tʰ], [n̥kʰ], [tsʼ], ...) which are allophonic/cluster realisations and are not separately spelled; they are therefore not emitted. (5) TONE: Oshindonga has two tones (high and low), but TONE IS NOT WRITTEN in the standard orthography and is therefore NOT recoverable from the text; no tone marks are emitted.

sources.author: Ager, S.
sources.title: Ndonga language, alphabet and pronunciation
sources.notes: Letter-to-IPA chart of the Ndonga alphabet: c=[ts], g=[ɣ], j=[dʒ], q=[kw], x=[x], y=[j], plus dh=[ð], th=[θ], sh=[ʃ], ng=[ŋ] and doubled long vowels.

sources.author: Fivaz, D.
sources.title: A Reference Grammar of Oshindonga
sources.notes: Reference grammar (2nd ed.); source cited by Wikipedia for the Oshindonga phoneme inventory and the two-tone system.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Ndonga — Phonology
sources.notes: Five-vowel system; consonant table incl. /ɲ ŋ θ ð ʃ ʒ x ɣ ts/; prenasalised [mb nd ŋɡ ndʒ] and compounds; 'Oshindonga has two tones: high and low.'

sources.author: Namibia. Ministry of Education, National Institute for Educational Development (NIED)
sources.title: Oshindonga: omunyolelo 3 / Oshindonga: Orthography 3
sources.notes: Current official standard orthography of Oshindonga (3rd edition) — the orthography assumed by this spec.

ancestors.notes: Atlantic-Congo — the classification node this language sits under.
